name: Ot-tjin
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Ot-tjin (Aw-li On-nam Ot-tjin): a relay-sowing 'make fish' Mancala of the Penihing people of Central Borneo, recorded by Lumholtz (1920). Two rows of nine holes plus a store at each end; 2-5 seeds per hole (3 common, 5 the challenging make-fish variant). Pick up a hole and sow clockwise; if the last seed lands in an occupied hole, lift it and sow again, on and on, until it lands in an empty hole (nothing caught) or brings a hole up to the starting count (a 'fish' -> your store). Cannot move and your opponent scoops the rest. Most fish wins; an equal catch is a draw (a genuine impasse is 'no result').
